name: Astragalus Huangqi Root Slices
description: One of the most essential qi-tonic adaptogens in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM). The root contains a dense profile of astragalosides and complex immunostimulant polysaccharides that stimulate T-cell activity and speed recovery from profound malnutrition fatigue.
preparation: Huangqi Decoction: Simmer 10g of dried root slices in 600ml of clean water for 30 minutes. Strain and administer 200ml warm daily.
safetyAlert: Exceptionally well tolerated with zero noted acute toxicities in traditional or clinical trials.
interactions: May reduce effectiveness of immunosuppressant drugs due to powerful immune-stimulating active constituents.
sideEffects: Rare mild bloating or transient warming flush when starting high doses.
contraindications: Active severe systemic autoimmune conditions (due to robust T-cell activation).
countyStorageAdvisory: Mombasa: Store root slices in sealed glass canisters with a food-grade desiccant pack to counteract humid air. Lodwar: Keep in double-walled paper bags inside a dry cupboard to block direct desert heat.
dosingRules: Children under 5: Simmer 2g in 100ml water (or porridge) daily for nutritional support. Adults: Simmer 10g to 15g of dried root slices in 600ml of water for 30-45 minutes. Strain and consume in divided doses throughout the day.
